title: Fronting and end-focus
explanation:
Fronting and end-focus

Speakers front information for emphasis and place new or important information at the end (end-focus).

  • What we need is more time. (fronted)
  • It was in 2019 that the law changed. (cleft for focus)
  • She bought a house in Lisbon. (new place at end)

questions.text: [*What concerns me most|Concerns me most what] is the lack of transparency.
It [*was in 2019 that|in 2019 was that|was that in 2019] the law finally changed.
[*A solution we urgently need|We urgently need a solution] is better training.
She met [*a renowned architect from Barcelona|from Barcelona a renowned architect met she] at the conference.
[*Particularly worrying|Worrying particularly] is the speed of change.

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 2
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option for each gap.

questions.text: ____ I cannot accept is deliberate misinformation. | *What | That | Which
It was the director ____ approved the budget. | *who | which | where
____ remarkable was their ability to adapt. | *Particularly | Rarely | Seldom
He gave the files to Maria, not to ____.  | *James | file | desk
[*The question we must ask|The question must we ask] is who benefits.
It [*is transparency that|transparency is that|is that transparency] builds trust.
[*Especially significant|Significant especially] was the role of local media.
They announced [*a major investment in renewable energy|in renewable energy a major investment announced they].
[*More pressing than ever|Pressing more than ever] is the need for reform.
